content.full_text:
February 2, 1978

Ms. Evelyn L. Roberson  
City Clerk  
City of South Lake Tahoe  
P.O. Box 1210  
South Lake Tahoe, California 95705

Dear Ms. Roberson:

In your letter of January 26, 1978, you ask whether a candidate for city office who has withdrawn from the campaign but whose name will appear, nonetheless, on the ballot for the March 7 municipal election must file campaign reports in connection with that election.

Section 84200 of the Government Code requires "each candidate" to file campaign statements not later than 40 days prior to the election, not later than 12 days prior to the election, and not later than 65 days after the election.

Section 82007 of the Government Code defines a "candidate" as "... an individual who is listed on the ballot ..."

Consequently, in the circumstances you have described the candidate who has withdrawn but whose name will appear on the March 7 ballot must file on the three occasions specified by Section 84200.

Sincerely yours,

John D. Giansello  
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

The message and reply are as follows:

MESSAGE

John Giannello  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
SACRAMENTO (CA) 95804  
DATE 1-26-78

(CANDIDATE WITHDRAWAL)

ATTACHED NEWSPAPER ARTICLE INDICATES A CANDIDATE HAS WITHDRAWN FROM THE RACE.

IS THERE ANY FILING REQUIREMENTS? SHE DID NOT FILE TODAY.

By Eleanor K. Robinson  
SIGNED
